  • Files
  • Examples

Standard I/O (stdio.h)
[C Library]

Detailed Description

The standard I/O library used by the BME runtime (as well as by other Tilera system software components, such as the Hypervisor).

This library is based upon the standard C I/O library but has limited functionality in some areas in order to meet space and implementation cost goals.

Defines

#define EOF   (-1)
 End of file.
#define getchar()   getc(stdin)
 Get a character from stdout.
#define putstr(s)   putstring((s), stdout)
 Write a string to stdout.

Typedefs

typedef struct _file FILE
 File structure.

Functions

int fprintf (FILE *f, const char *fmt,...)
 Print to a file.
int printf (const char *fmt,...)
 Print to stdout.
int tprintf (const char *fmt,...)
 Print to stdout, prepending tile coordinates ("(x,y) ").
int utprintf (const char *fmt,...)
 Print to stdout, prepending tile coordinates ("(x,y) ").
int vfprintf (FILE *stream, const char *fmt, va_list ap)
 Print to a file.
int vprintf (const char *fmt, va_list ap)
 Print to stdout.
int snprintf (char *str, size_t size, const char *fmt,...)
 Print to a limited-length string.
int vsnprintf (char *str, size_t size, const char *fmt, va_list ap)
 Print to a limited-length string.
int puts (const char *s)
 Write a string, followed by a newline, to stdout.
int fputs (const char *s, FILE *f)
 Write a string to the named file.
char * fgets (char *s, int len, FILE *f)
 Read a string from the named file.
int fflush (FILE *f)
 Flush any data in the file's buffer to its eventual destination.
int ffsync (FILE *f)
 Block until any data written to this file has been emitted to the outside world.
long ftell (FILE *f)
 Return the current file position.
int ungetc (int c, FILE *f)
 Push a character back onto a file.
static void flockfile (FILE *f)
 Lock a file.
static void funlockfile (FILE *f)
 Unlock a file.
static int putc (int c, FILE *f)
 Write a character to a stream.
static int getc (FILE *f)
 Get a character from a stream.
int putchar (int c)
 Write a character to stdout.
int fputc (int c, FILE *f)
 Write a character to a file.
void putstring (const char *s, FILE *f)
 Write a string to a file.

Variables

FILE bme_stdout_null
 Null output file.
FILE bme_stdout_sim
 Simulator output file.
FILE bme_stdout_hvcons
 Hypervisor console output file.
FILE * stdout
 Default standard output file.
FILE * stdin
 Default standard input file.

Define Documentation

#define EOF   (-1)

End of file.

 
#define getchar (  )     getc(stdin)

Get a character from stdout.

#define putstr ( s   )     putstring((s), stdout)

Write a string to stdout.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct _file FILE

File structure.

Represents an input or output stream.

Function Documentation

int fprintf ( FILE *  f,
const char *  fmt,
  ... 
)

Print to a file.

Parameters:
f File to which output will be directed.
fmt Format string; see printf for details.
Returns:
Number of characters printed.
Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

int printf ( const char *  fmt,
  ... 
)

Print to stdout.

Parameters:
fmt Format string. This printf supports the d, i, u, p, x, X, o, c, and s descriptors, and the "#", "0", "-", " ", "+", and "'" flags. Descriptors can take a width, but specifying a precision is not supported, except that the .* precision specifier is supported for s only. Floating-point output is not supported.
Returns:
Number of characters printed.

int tprintf ( const char *  fmt,
  ... 
)

Print to stdout, prepending tile coordinates ("(x,y) ").

Parameters:
fmt Format string; see printf for details.
Returns:
Number of characters printed.
Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

int utprintf ( const char *  fmt,
  ... 
)

Print to stdout, prepending tile coordinates ("(x,y) ").

This is exactly the same as tprintf except its arguments are not checked at compile time. This allows using the "%'x" extension that gcc would normally warn about. Because it stifles useful warnings, this function should only be used when absolutely necessary.

Parameters:
fmt Format string; see printf for details.
Returns:
Number of characters printed.

int vfprintf ( FILE *  stream,
const char *  fmt,
va_list  ap 
)

Print to a file.

Parameters:
stream File to which output will be directed.
fmt Format string; see printf for details.
ap Items to be formatted.
Returns:
Number of characters printed.

int vprintf ( const char *  fmt,
va_list  ap 
)

Print to stdout.

Parameters:
fmt Format string; see printf for details.
ap Items to be formatted.
Returns:
Number of characters printed.

int snprintf ( char *  str,
size_t  size,
const char *  fmt,
  ... 
)

Print to a limited-length string.

Parameters:
str String to which output will be directed.
size String length.
fmt Format string; see printf for details.
Returns:
Number of characters, not including the trailing NULL, which would have been printed if the string were long enough to hold them. If the string actually was long enough, this is the number printed.

int vsnprintf ( char *  str,
size_t  size,
const char *  fmt,
va_list  ap 
)

Print to a limited-length string.

Parameters:
str String to which output will be directed.
size String length.
fmt Format string; see printf for details.
ap Items to be formatted.
Returns:
Number of characters, not including the trailing NULL, which would have been printed if the string were long enough to hold them. If the string actually was long enough, this is the number printed.

int puts ( const char *  s  ) 

Write a string, followed by a newline, to stdout.

Parameters:
s String to write.

int fputs ( const char *  s,
FILE *  f 
)

Write a string to the named file.

Parameters:
s String to write.
f File to which output will be directed.

char* fgets ( char *  s,
int  len,
FILE *  f 
)

Read a string from the named file.

Parameters:
s Buffer to fill.
len Length of buffer.
f File from which input will be taken.

int fflush ( FILE *  f  ) 

Flush any data in the file's buffer to its eventual destination.

Parameters:
f File to flush.

int ffsync ( FILE *  f  ) 

Block until any data written to this file has been emitted to the outside world.

Different from fflush() in that the former may return when data has been committed to a device's output buffer but not actually transmitted. Different from the Unix fsync() system call since it takes a FILE*, not a file descriptor.

Parameters:
f File to flush.

long ftell ( FILE *  f  ) 

Return the current file position.

Parameters:
f File to inspect.
Returns:
Number of bytes read or written since the file was initialized.

int ungetc ( int  c,
FILE *  f 
)

Push a character back onto a file.

Parameters:
c Character to push back.
f File upon which the character will be pushed back.
Returns:
The character pushed back, or EOF on error.

static void flockfile ( FILE *  f  )  [inline, static]

Lock a file.

static void funlockfile ( FILE *  f  )  [inline, static]

Unlock a file.

static int putc ( int  c,
FILE *  f 
) [inline, static]

Write a character to a stream.

static int getc ( FILE *  f  )  [inline, static]

Get a character from a stream.

int putchar ( int  c  ) 

Write a character to stdout.

int fputc ( int  c,
FILE *  f 
)

Write a character to a file.

void putstring ( const char *  s,
FILE *  f 
)

Write a string to a file.

Parameters:
s String to write.
f File to which output will be directed.

Variable Documentation

FILE bme_stdout_null

Null output file.

FILE bme_stdout_sim

Simulator output file.

FILE bme_stdout_hvcons

Hypervisor console output file.

FILE* stdout

Default standard output file.

Under the BME, this is set by default to bme_stdout_hvcons, which sends output to the hypervisor console.

FILE* stdin

Default standard input file.

Under the BME, this set by default to bme_stdin_null, which always returns EOF; no other input streams are currently supported.
